value_picks.name: Accenture plc

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $12.16B / mcap $109.5B = 11.1% yield, comfortably above the 7% pass bar.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 11.94 vs implied PE 16.67 (target median $248.50 / fwd EPS $14.91) = 28.4% discount, just under the 30% pass threshold.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash position: $9.41B cash vs $8.35B debt (+$1.06B). D/E 25.5 and current ratio 1.336 also clear the strict pass criteria.

dividend.detail: Dividend yield 3.62% with payout ratio 50.98% — well-covered and above 2%.

institutional.detail: Institutions hold 83.1%, recommendation_mean 1.86 (Buy), 18 of 28 analysts at Buy/Strong Buy (64%).

ai_exposure.detail: Accenture is a primary GenAI integrator/services provider for enterprises; AI consulting bookings are a structural growth driver despite near-term federal-spending and discretionary-tech-spending headwinds compressing the multiple.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest jumped 22.5% MoM to 3.27% but base level is still low; not a meaningful crowded-short setup. Deep multi-year drawdown (-45%) on a profitable, FCF-rich consultancy with a 3.6% yield is the headline 'deal' here.

catalysts_general: Trading at 28% discount to analyst median target ($248.50, +40% upside)
FCF yield of 11.1% on a high-ROE (24.8%) services franchise
45% drawdown from 52w high with price near 52w low — sentiment-driven sell-off
Net cash balance sheet with 3.62% dividend yield well-covered at 51% payout

downside_scenarios.scenario: Re-test 52w low zone if federal IT spending cuts deepen; FCF yield rises to ~12%.
GenAI services growth disappoints AND consulting bookings flatten — historical trough multiple for ACN.
Recession + AI deflation thesis — 25% further downside; dividend yield approaches 4.8%.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Limit just above 52w low ($172.52). Entering near support with an 11x fwd PE and 11%+ FCF yield offers asymmetric reward; below this level signals fundamental thesis change (federal cuts spilling broadly).

comparables.note: In input — cheaper but lower-quality franchise (lower margins, lower ROE).
Not in input — IT services/consulting peer, trades at premium on AI-software story.
Not in input — Indian IT services peer, similar end markets but USD-translation tailwind.

value_picks.analyst_take: Best risk/reward of the six on the framework — 5.5/6 with a high-quality franchise trading at a 45% drawdown and 11% FCF yield. The federal/discretionary spending overhang is real but the price already discounts a meaningful slowdown, and the net cash + 3.6% covered dividend cushions the wait. BUY with sizing into 52w low support.

value_picks.name: Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $1.91B / mcap $24.37B = 7.83% yield, just above the 7% pass bar.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 8.37 vs implied PE 11.85 (target median $73 / fwd EPS $6.16) = 29.4% discount, just under 30% pass threshold.

drawdown.detail: Trading 40.91% below 52w high of $87.03; price within $1.25 of 52w low.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash: $1.52B cash vs $1.09B debt (+$425M). D/E very low at 7.25, current ratio robust at 2.23.

dividend.detail: 2.54% yield with conservative 27.3% payout — substantial coverage room.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership reported as 105.5% (data quirk — effectively saturated), recommendation_mean 2.17 (Buy).

ai_exposure.detail: AI is a double-edged sword for CTSH — same opportunity as ACN to deliver AI integration services, but lower-tier brand positioning and weaker margins (15.6% op vs ACN's 13.8% but on lower revenue/employee leverage) make it a price-taker rather than price-maker. Risk that AI compresses traditional ADM/IT outsourcing revenue is real.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 7.55% with 6.6 days-to-cover is the highest of the six and rose 14.6% MoM — meaningful bearish positioning. Combined with deep drawdown and net cash, this is a contrarian setup if Q1 earnings hold the line on bookings.

catalysts_general: Trading at 30% discount to analyst median target ($73.00, +42% upside)
Single-digit forward PE (8.37x) on a profitable services company with $1.9B FCF
Price within 1% of 52w low — capitulation territory
PEG ratio 0.84 implies cheap relative to forward growth
Net cash plus highest current ratio of group (2.23)

downside_scenarios.scenario: 52w low breach on continued IT services slowdown narrative.
Bookings deteriorate; AI cannibalization of staff-augmentation revenue accelerates.
Recession + structural AI deflation — historical trough for CTSH multiple; FCF yield ~12%.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Limit just above 52w low ($50.19). Stock is already in capitulation zone; small additional pullback on a market wobble offers a sub-8x fwd PE entry with 7%+ FCF yield and net cash to back it.

comparables.note: In input — premium peer, higher margins/ROE; CTSH discount is structural (~30%) but widened recently.
Not in input — direct Indian-heritage IT services peer, much higher multiple.
Not in input — global IT services peer, similar end-market exposure.

value_picks.analyst_take: Cheapest of the six (8.4x fwd PE, 7.8% FCF yield) on a fortress balance sheet, and at the 52w low. The 7.5% short interest and rising trend signals real bearish thesis (AI-deflation of traditional IT services), but 5.5/6 framework score with a 2.5% covered yield earns a BUY at this price — risk/reward is favorable if Q1 doesn't reset bookings expectations lower.

value_picks.name: Workday, Inc.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 10.09 vs implied PE 14.26 (target median $177.50 / fwd EPS $12.45) = 29.2% discount, just under 30%.

drawdown.detail: Trading 54.62% below 52w high of $276.00 — deepest drawdown in the group.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $1.62B ($5.44B cash vs $3.82B debt). D/E 48.96 (just under 50 threshold), current ratio 1.32 — net cash branch satisfies the strict pass criterion.

dividend.detail: No dividend (payout 0%) — neutral per framework, scored as 0.5.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership 100.3%, recommendation_mean 1.95 (Buy), 25 of 41 analysts at Buy/Strong Buy (61%).

ai_exposure.detail: Workday's HCM/financials platform is a primary surface for embedded AI agents (workflow automation, recruiting copilots, expense fraud detection). Customer-base of 11K+ enterprises with sticky data make it a credible AI-distribution vector — but valuation expansion case depends on monetizing AI SKUs at attach rates not yet evidenced in revenue mix.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 9.23% with 23.9% MoM increase is the most bearish positioning in the group. Forward PE 10x on a 14.5% revenue grower is extraordinary if real; the gap between trailing PE 48.5x and forward PE 10x suggests a one-time tax benefit or stock-based comp reclassification baking in the forward EPS — verify before treating as base case.

catalysts_general: Trading at 29% discount to analyst median target ($177.50, +42% upside)
PEG ratio 0.47 — cheapest relative to growth in the group
Revenue growth 14.5% with 75.7% gross margins — best growth-quality combination of the six
54.6% drawdown from high — capitulation on a Rule of 40 software franchise
Earnings growth 62.9% on operating leverage from cost discipline

downside_scenarios.scenario: Re-test 52w low ($110.36) on enterprise IT spending freeze.
Material guide-down; SaaS multiple compression; tests pre-pandemic levels.
Recession + competitive concession to ServiceNow/SAP at HCM upmarket.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Limit just above 52w low. The forward PE math (10x) is too cheap to be the right number for a 14% grower — the optical discount likely reflects skepticism on forward EPS quality. Entering at 52w low gives a margin of safety on the optical case while reserving WATCHLIST status until next earnings clarifies forward EPS bridge.

comparables.note: In input — similar fwd PE / growth profile, but higher margins and a price closer to its peer-history range.
Not in input — direct enterprise SaaS peer, premium multiple.
Not in input — workflow SaaS peer trading at 5x WDAY's multiple.

value_picks.analyst_take: WDAY screens like the most asymmetric setup in the group — 14.5% growth at 10x fwd PE is anomalous and points to either deep mispricing or a forward-EPS quality issue worth verifying. Framework score of 5.0/6 with deepest drawdown and best growth, but no dividend cushion and the highest short-interest growth (+24%) keeps it at WATCHLIST until earnings de-risk forward EPS.

value_picks.name: Leidos Holdings, Inc.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 9.76 vs implied PE 14.81 (target median $195 / fwd EPS $13.17) = 34.1% discount, clearing the 30% threshold.

drawdown.detail: Trading 37.67% below 52w high of $205.77; price near 52w low.

balance_sheet.detail: Net debt position: $457M cash vs $6.94B debt (-$6.49B). D/E 137.1 (in 50-150 borderline band), current ratio 1.397 (>0.8 borderline criterion).

dividend.detail: 1.31% yield with very conservative 15.2% payout — yield below 2% threshold for full pass but well-covered.

institutional.detail: Institutions 81.5%, recommendation_mean 2.13 (Buy), 8 of 16 analysts at Buy/Strong Buy (50%).

ai_exposure.detail: Leidos has direct exposure to defense/intel AI contracts (warfighter ML, autonomous systems, cyber). Federal AI spending is one of the few protected line items in current budget pressure scenarios — provides differentiated upside vs commercial IT services peers.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest is the lowest in the group (3.17%) and decreased 7.5% MoM — no crowded-short. Beta of 0.57 is by far the lowest, reflecting government-revenue stability. The DOGE/federal-spending narrative is the dominant overhang.

catalysts_general: Trading at 34% discount to analyst median target ($195, +52% upside)
Price within $0.40 of 52w low — capitulation on federal-spending-cut fears
ROE 30.6% — highest in the group, driven by leverage but real
Beta 0.57 — defensive characteristics rare in tech-sector classification
Earnings down 7.6% YoY — the only earnings-decliner of the six

downside_scenarios.scenario: DOGE federal spending review hits a Leidos contract; tests pre-2024 lows.
Multiple program cuts; debt-load forces dividend cut consideration.
Major federal IT consolidation; 7x debt/EBITDA stress on $6.9B debt becomes binding.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Wait for break/hold of 52w low ($127.87) as a confirmation level. The $6.9B debt vs $457M cash makes this NOT a 'buy any drawdown' name — federal-budget tail risk is real and the balance sheet has limited cushion to absorb a contract loss. Better to be patient than aggressive here.

comparables.note: Not in input — direct federal IT/consulting peer at premium multiple.
Not in input — closest direct comp; LDOS trades at meaningful discount.
Not in input — federal IT peer; LDOS discount reflects margin/contract mix differences.

value_picks.analyst_take: Optically the cheapest defensive name (beta 0.57, 34% target discount) but the heaviest balance-sheet risk in the group (D/E 137, $6.9B net debt) makes the federal-spending-cut tail dangerous. WATCHLIST — the framework score of 5.0/6 plus negative earnings growth and a borderline balance sheet earns patience over conviction; ideal trade is on confirmation that 52w low holds and Q1 bookings de-risk the federal pipeline.

value_picks.name: Adobe Inc.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 9.48 vs implied PE 11.93 (target median $314.76 / fwd EPS $26.39) = 20.5% discount — in the 15-30% borderline band.

balance_sheet.detail: Marginally net cash ($6.90B cash vs $6.67B debt = +$229M, ~0.2% of mcap). Current ratio 0.912 (below 1.0) and D/E 58.3 are both borderline; the net-cash buffer is essentially zero. Marking borderline reflects the substance, not the literal sign.

dividend.detail: No dividend (payout 0%) — neutral per framework, scored as 0.5.

institutional.detail: Institutions 87.1%, recommendation_mean 2.46 (just under 2.5 Buy threshold), 16 of 39 analysts at Buy/Strong Buy (41%) — passes both gates but the rec_mean is the weakest of the group.

ai_exposure.detail: Adobe faces the most explicit AI-disruption thesis of the six: GenAI image/video models (Midjourney, Sora, Runway) directly attack Photoshop/Premiere moats, and Figma + AI-native creative tools chip at the high end. Adobe's Firefly response is real but margin-dilutive vs the legacy subscription mix. The threat IS the discount.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 4.07% with 11.7% MoM increase — modestly rising but not crowded. The fundamental setup is a 40% drawdown on a 30% net-margin franchise; the question is whether AI is a temporary discount or a terminal-value reset.

catalysts_general: Trading at 21% discount to analyst median target ($314.76, +26% upside)
Forward PE 9.48 on a 29.5% net-margin / 58.8% ROE franchise — cheapest Adobe has been ex-2008
PEG 0.71 on 12% revenue / 11% earnings growth
40.9% drawdown reflects real terminal-value debate, not just cyclical fade
89.4% gross margins — best in the group

downside_scenarios.scenario: Test 52w low on AI-disruption headline / Figma-style competitive announcement.
Multi-quarter ARR deceleration confirms structural pricing pressure.
AI-native creative tools take material share; Adobe's terminal-growth thesis is reset to mid-single-digits.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Limit just above 52w low ($224.13). At 8.5x fwd PE on a 30% margin franchise the AI-disruption discount becomes asymmetric, but waiting for that level is critical — at $250 you're paying for the AI threat to NOT materialize, while at $225 you're paid for it.

comparables.note: In input — premium peer, more durable AI moat (tax / accounting).
Not in input — software platform peer, AI-distribution beneficiary, no drawdown.
Not in input — direct AI-native creative competitor; the bear case for ADBE.

value_picks.analyst_take: Cheapest Adobe has traded ex-crisis on the optical multiple, but the AI-disruption threat is genuinely structural rather than cyclical, which is why the framework score drops to 4.5 (no dividend, marginal balance sheet, weakest analyst rec_mean of group). WATCHLIST with a strict 52w-low entry — the math says BUY at $225, but paying $250 for the AI-threat-disproven scenario is a bet against a real headwind.

value_picks.name: Intuit Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $5.30B / mcap $108.96B = 4.87% yield — in the 4-7% borderline band.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 14.79 vs implied PE 22.28 (target median $590 / fwd EPS $26.48) = 33.6% discount, clearing the 30% threshold.

drawdown.detail: Trading 52.02% below 52w high of $813.70 — second-deepest drawdown in the group.

balance_sheet.detail: Net debt $3.91B ($2.98B cash vs $6.89B debt) but D/E 36.1 (<50) and current ratio 1.32 (>1.2) cleanly pass the secondary criterion.

dividend.detail: 1.14% yield with conservative 29.2% payout — yield below 2% threshold for full pass but well-covered.

institutional.detail: Institutions 91.0%, recommendation_mean 1.47 — strongest analyst conviction in the group, 29 of 34 at Buy/Strong Buy (85%).

ai_exposure.detail: Intuit's tax/accounting/credit data assets are extremely defensible against GenAI — competing requires regulatory expertise, IRS data partnerships, and decades of edge cases in TurboTax/QuickBooks. AI is a margin tailwind (Intuit Assist deflects support cost) more than a revenue threat. The 52% drawdown is a multiple reset, not an AI-disruption thesis.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 3.9% with 30.1% MoM increase — biggest short-interest growth in the group, suggesting traders fading the bounce attempt. Highest analyst conviction (rec_mean 1.47) of the six against rising shorts is a notable divergence to monitor.

catalysts_general: Trading at 34% discount to analyst median target ($590, +51% upside)
Highest revenue growth (17.4%) and earnings growth (48.5%) in the group
Strongest analyst conviction in the group (rec_mean 1.47, 85% Buy/Strong Buy)
52% drawdown from a name with 21.6% net margins and 23.5% ROE
Forward PE 14.8 vs trailing 25.5 — sharp re-rating already priced in

downside_scenarios.scenario: Re-test 52w low on consumer/SMB spending pullback or IRS Direct File expansion concerns.
Tax season miss; multi-year multiple compression to deep-recession troughs.
Material guide-down + IRS Direct File takes share faster than expected; Credit Karma deteriorates.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Limit just above 52w low ($342.11). Forward PE compresses to 13x at that level — historical floor for INTU and a level where the 17% growth + analyst conviction combination becomes very hard to fade. The 4.9% FCF yield only clears the borderline bar; you want a wider margin of safety on the entry.

comparables.note: In input — software peer; INTU growth and analyst conviction are stronger but valuation is higher.
Not in input — large-cap software peer at much higher multiple.
Not in input — SMB-services peer, lower growth at premium multiple.

value_picks.analyst_take: Best-quality franchise of the six but priciest entry on FCF-yield (4.9% just clears the borderline bar) — the 52% drawdown is from frothy 2024 highs rather than a structural reset. WATCHLIST with a 52w-low entry trigger ($345); at that level the framework arguably moves to 5.5/6 and the highest-conviction-analyst setup with structural AI-defensibility becomes a clear BUY.

growth_picks.name: Newmont Corporation

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 45.8% — well above 30% threshold, exceptional for a gold major driven by gold price strength and Newcrest integration synergies.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margin 67.4% — above 65% threshold; reflects high gold price leverage on relatively fixed mining cost base.

rule_of_40.detail: Rule of 40 = 45.8 + 61.4 = 107.2 — far above 50 threshold; operating margins 61.4% are extraordinary for a miner.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 78.6% > revenue growth 45.8% — clear operating leverage as gold price rises drop disproportionately to the bottom line.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 25.8% — above 20% threshold; capital-intensive miner producing strong returns on equity at current gold prices.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 2.03% (<5%) AND declining 10.55% MoM — bearish bets being unwound.

ai_exposure.detail: Gold mining has no direct AI exposure as tailwind or threat; secondary benefit from AI-enabled mine optimization but immaterial to thesis. Gold itself benefits indirectly as an inflation/debasement hedge against AI-driven fiscal/monetary regimes.

deal_radar.assessment: FCF $9.8B against $5.5B debt and $8.8B cash — net cash position. With low payout ratio of 13.1% and dividend yield 0.92%, ample firepower for buybacks, M&A in copper/critical minerals, or accelerated debt reduction.

catalysts_general: Trading 24.7% below mean analyst target of $144.01 and 21.7% below median $147.50
Forward P/E of 10.16x against 25.8% ROE and 45.8% revenue growth — deep value relative to growth profile
20 analysts cover with recommendation_mean 1.62 (between Strong Buy and Buy); 17/21 are Buy/Strong Buy
Short interest declining 10.55% MoM signals improving sentiment
Beta 0.448 — defensive position with growth-rate fundamentals

downside_scenarios.scenario: Gold price pullback 10% from current levels compresses margins; stock tests SMA support zone.
Broader risk-off + gold mean reversion to $2,500/oz; stock retraces to mid-2025 base.
Match analyst low target — recession-driven gold price collapse below $2,200/oz combined with operational miss.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Slightly below SMA20 ($113.06) and SMA50 ($112.28) — provides ~5% margin of safety against current price while still capturing upside to $144 mean target (~31% upside). Avoids paying full markup.

comparables.note: Barrick Gold — peer major; trades at similar multiple but lacks Newmont's Newcrest-driven growth acceleration.
Agnico Eagle — premium peer; richer multiple reflects better operational record but NEM offers more upside on multiple expansion.
Gold Fields — South African major; not in input data, included for sector context.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Newmont is the rare 6/6 framework score outside of pure software — gold price tailwind is dropping straight to a 78.6% earnings growth rate against a deep-value 10.2x forward multiple. With short interest declining and analyst consensus 25% above current price, this is a classic mispriced cyclical with defensive beta (0.45). Risk is gold price reversion if real yields spike.

growth_picks.name: Applovin Corporation

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 59% — nearly 2x the 30% threshold; AXON ad-tech engine driving extraordinary acceleration.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margin 88.4% — software-economics levels; well above 65% threshold.

rule_of_40.detail: Rule of 40 = 59 + 78.2 = 137.2 — extraordinary; operating margin 78.2% indicates a near-monopoly economic profile in mobile ad-tech.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 113% > revenue growth 59% — strong operating leverage as fixed AI infrastructure scales against expanding ad spend.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 266% — extreme due to low book equity ($6.31/sh) from buybacks; reflects exceptional capital efficiency though distorted by negative retained earnings dynamics.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 4.26% (<5%) AND declining 5.03% MoM — shorts capitulating after the recent rally from $320.

ai_exposure.detail: AppLovin's entire AXON ad engine is AI-native; the company is one of the clearest beneficiaries of generative AI in ad targeting and creative optimization. Earnings acceleration to 113% is largely attributable to AXON 2.0 model improvements.

deal_radar.assessment: FCF $3.1B with debt $3.85B (D/E 162.9% reflects buyback-driven equity reduction). Cash $2.76B. Has been an aggressive buyer of own shares — float 241M vs shares out 305.7M shows insider/strategic concentration.

catalysts_general: Trading 38% below 52-week high $745.61 — significant drawdown despite improving fundamentals
Mean analyst target $639.57 implies 38% upside; median $652 implies 41% upside
Forward P/E 21.3x against 59% revenue growth and 113% earnings growth = PEG well below 1 (0.19 on earnings growth)
Recent momentum strong: +11% in 20d, +6.6% in 50d — recovery underway
26/30 analyst ratings are Buy/Strong Buy; recommendation_mean 1.47

downside_scenarios.scenario: Retest of recent base; broader ad-tech multiple compression on macro slowdown.
Match analyst low target — TikTok ban deal complications, AXON growth deceleration concerns.
Revisit 52-week low — material AXON model performance degradation or ad market recession.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just below SMA20 ($462.63) — captures a small pullback within the active uptrend (SMA50 $445.74 acts as support). Asymmetric setup with 38%+ upside to mean target vs ~4% downside cushion.

comparables.note: Meta — broader ad-tech comp; APP trades at similar multiple but with 3x the growth.
The Trade Desk — pure-play ad-tech; richer multiple at lower growth, suggesting APP is undervalued (not in input).
Alphabet — incumbent ad platform; APP's growth profile dwarfs at similar multiple (not in input).

growth_picks.analyst_take: AppLovin is a 6/6 framework name with a PEG ratio of 1.31 against 113% earnings growth — the multiple has not caught up to the AXON-driven fundamentals after a 38% drawdown. The combination of recovering price action, declining short interest, and 38% analyst upside makes this an asymmetric setup. Key risk is AXON growth normalizing back toward 30-40% rather than sustaining 60%+.

growth_picks.name: Oracle Corporation

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 21.7% — within 15-30% borderline band; respectable acceleration for a mature enterprise software vendor driven by OCI/AI infrastructure.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margin 67.1% — above 65% threshold; legacy software economics preserved despite OCI capex drag.

rule_of_40.detail: Rule of 40 = 21.7 + 32.68 = 54.4 — above 50 threshold but not by much; operating margin 32.7% reflects the heavy AI capex investment cycle.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 24.5% > revenue growth 21.7% — modest operating leverage; not the explosive acceleration of pure-play AI names but consistent.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 57.6% — far above 20% threshold; aided by leveraged capital structure (D/E 415%) but reflects strong returns on equity.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 1.71% (<5%) AND declining 3.97% MoM — minimal short pressure on this large-cap.

ai_exposure.detail: Oracle has emerged as the surprise AI infrastructure winner — major Stargate/OpenAI compute deal and rapidly growing OCI backlog. The negative FCF (-$22.3B) is precisely the cost of front-loading data center buildout for AI workloads, which the market is clearly rewarding (+25.7% in 90d).

deal_radar.assessment: FCF deeply negative at -$22.3B reflects aggressive AI infrastructure capex. Total debt $162.2B vs cash $39.1B — leveraged balance sheet but enterprise contract backlog provides counter-collateral. Debt/equity 415% is concerning if AI capex doesn't monetize as projected.

catalysts_general: Down 43% from 52-week high $345.72 — sharp correction from peak euphoria, but momentum re-accelerating (+26.5% in 20d, +35.4% in 50d)
Mean analyst target $242.10 implies 23% upside; median $220 implies 12%
Forward P/E 24.4x against 24.5% earnings growth = PEG ~1.0 — fairly priced
36/43 analyst ratings are Buy/Strong Buy; recommendation_mean 1.56
Stargate AI compute deal wins position Oracle as third-pillar hyperscaler
Negative FCF of $22.3B is the smoking gun of AI buildout investment

downside_scenarios.scenario: Pullback to SMA50 area (~$160) extension; AI multiple compression as hyperscaler capex digestion concerns intensify.
Match analyst low target — material AI customer churn or backlog conversion delay.
Revisit 52-week low — broad cloud-AI bubble unwind; balance sheet leverage becomes a real concern given negative FCF.

ideal_entry.reasoning: The recent 26.5% rally in 20 days has stretched the chart well above SMA20 ($176.24) and SMA50 ($159.83). A pullback toward $175 would still leave you above the 50-day and offer better risk/reward to the $242 mean target. Chasing here on momentum is expensive given the leveraged balance sheet.

comparables.note: Microsoft — premier AI/cloud peer; richer multiple but less leveraged balance sheet (not in input).
Alphabet — comparable hyperscaler; cheaper multiple at lower growth, no debt concern (not in input).
Amazon AWS — incumbent cloud leader; ORCL trades at discount but with execution risk on AI buildout (not in input).

growth_picks.analyst_take: Oracle scores 5.5/6 but the chart has run hard — up 35.4% in 50 days and now 26.5% above SMA20. The AI infrastructure thesis is real (negative $22.3B FCF is the proof, not a flaw) but the leveraged balance sheet (D/E 415%) means execution must be perfect. WATCHLIST until a pullback to $175-$180 offers better entry with the AI capex story still intact.

growth_picks.name: Teradyne, Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 87% — far above 30% threshold; massive semiconductor test cycle recovery driven by AI chip volume.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margin 58.7% — within 50-65% borderline band; lower than software peers due to hardware/equipment business model but solid for semicap.

rule_of_40.detail: Rule of 40 = 87 + 37.6 = 124.6 — exceptional; operating margin 37.6% impressive given equipment cost structure.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 314.8% >> revenue growth 87% — extreme operating leverage as test equipment cycle inflects from depressed base.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 28.75% — above 20% threshold; clean balance sheet (D/E 2.6%) makes the return on equity organic.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 3.62% (<5%) AND declining 5.32% MoM — shorts unwinding the cycle-bear thesis.

ai_exposure.detail: Teradyne is a direct AI infrastructure play — every advanced AI chip (NVDA H100/B200, AMD MI300, custom hyperscaler ASICs) requires test equipment. The 87% revenue growth and 315% earnings growth precisely reflect AI-driven test intensity. Robotics segment adds secondary AI/automation exposure.

deal_radar.assessment: FCF $298M is modest relative to $56B market cap (FCF yield ~0.5%) — investors paying for the future earnings ramp. Pristine balance sheet ($245M cash vs $82M debt). Low payout ratio 9.1% leaves room for buybacks once cycle stabilizes.

catalysts_general: Down 15% from 52-week high $422.11 after 5x rally from $76 low — healthy consolidation in uptrend
Mean analyst target $369.53 implies modest 3% upside; median $387 implies 8% — analysts may be lagging the cycle
Forward P/E 37.6x is rich, but PEG 1.51 reasonable given 87% revenue growth
Earnings acceleration of 315% is the key signal — operating leverage off depressed base
Held 97% by institutions — high quality holder base
12/18 analyst ratings are Buy/Strong Buy; recommendation_mean 1.72

downside_scenarios.scenario: Pullback to SMA50 ($332.63) area — typical mid-cycle digestion; AI chip volume forecast trim.
Match analyst low target — material slowdown in AI chip orders or competitive loss of market share to ADI/Cohu.
Cyclical reversal — semiconductor test cycle peaks early; multiple compression to historic mid-cycle levels.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near SMA50 ($332.63) — recent pullback below SMA20 ($368.66) suggests near-term cooling; SMA50 has held as support during the uptrend. Better risk/reward against $369 mean target rather than chasing here.

comparables.note: Applied Materials — broader semicap; cheaper multiple but slower growth profile (not in input).
Lam Research — etch/dep peer; cheaper but also slower (not in input).
KLA — process control peer; closer comp to Teradyne's test niche, also cheaper (not in input).

growth_picks.analyst_take: Teradyne is the sneaky AI infrastructure play — 87% revenue growth and 315% earnings growth reflect direct exposure to advanced AI chip test intensity. The 5/5.5 framework score is strong but the multiple (37.6x forward) is the highest in the semicap group, reflecting the cycle inflection. Worth waiting for a pullback to SMA50 ($335) for better entry rather than chasing the recent +12% in 50 days.

growth_picks.name: Palantir Technologies Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 84.7% — far above 30% threshold; commercial AIP adoption driving the acceleration.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margin 84.1% — software-economics; well above 65% threshold.

rule_of_40.detail: Rule of 40 = 84.7 + 46.2 = 130.9 — extraordinary; operating margin 46.2% rare for a hyper-growth software company.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 325% >> revenue growth 84.7% — extreme operating leverage as AIP commercial deals scale on existing R&D base.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 32.6% — above 20% threshold; clean balance sheet ($8B cash vs $212M debt) makes returns organic, not leveraged.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 2.48% (<5%) but RISING +1.06% MoM — does not meet 'declining AND <5%' for pass; qualifies for borderline since <10%.

ai_exposure.detail: Palantir is among the purest AI software plays — AIP (Artificial Intelligence Platform) is the primary growth engine, with bootcamps converting Fortune 500 enterprises rapidly. Government segment also benefits from AI-driven defense modernization. The 84.7% revenue growth and 325% earnings growth are AI-narrative dependent.

deal_radar.assessment: FCF $1.75B against minimal debt $212M and cash $8B — fortress balance sheet. Current ratio 6.9. No buyback program despite cash hoard suggests management may pursue M&A or strategic investments.

catalysts_general: Down 35% from 52-week high $207.52 — meaningful drawdown but still up materially YoY
Mean analyst target $181.73 implies 34% upside; median $190 implies 40%
Forward P/E 65.9x is steep but PEG 2.1 manageable given growth profile
Price/Sales 62.2x is the highest multiple in this cohort by a wide margin — premium baked in
Trading below both SMA20 ($141.12) and SMA50 ($145.66) — short-term technical weakness
Analyst sentiment most cautious of the AI cohort: only 19/31 Buy/Strong Buy with 2 Sell/Strong Sell
Short interest rising despite strong fundamentals — bears finding entry

downside_scenarios.scenario: Test of 52-week low ($114.90) area — multiple compression as AI hype cycle cools.
Material AIP growth deceleration; rotation out of AI premium names to value.
Match analyst low target — AI bubble unwind; multiple normalizes toward enterprise software comps (~30x at mature growth).

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just above 52-week low ($114.90) — technical setup is broken (below SMA20 and SMA50, short interest rising). Better to wait for a retest of the prior low with confirmation rather than catch a falling knife at 65x forward earnings. At $118 the upside to $181 target becomes a much more attractive 54% with floor support nearby.

comparables.note: AppLovin (in this cohort) — similar AI-software profile at one-third the multiple of PLTR; suggests PLTR is overvalued relative to peers.
CrowdStrike — premium SaaS comp; similar multiple but slower growth makes PLTR's PEG more attractive (not in input).
Snowflake — even richer multiple at slower growth; PLTR looks reasonable in context (not in input).

growth_picks.analyst_take: Palantir scores 5.5/6 on fundamentals but trades at a 62x P/S that prices in perfection — and the technical setup just broke (below SMA20 and SMA50, short interest now rising). The 325% earnings growth is real but unsustainable; expect deceleration. WATCHLIST at $135; wait for retest of $115-$120 zone for a multiple that better reflects normalized growth.

growth_picks.name: Eli Lilly and Company

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 55.5% — far above 30% threshold; GLP-1 franchise (Mounjaro/Zepbound) driving extraordinary growth for a $850B large-cap pharma.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margin 82.8% — branded pharma economics; well above 65% threshold.

rule_of_40.detail: Rule of 40 = 55.5 + 49.4 = 104.9 — exceptional for a pharma; operating margin 49.4% reflects GLP-1 pricing power.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 169.9% >> revenue growth 55.5% — strong operating leverage as GLP-1 manufacturing capacity ramps against fixed R&D base.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 107.5% — far above 20% threshold; reflects both strong operating returns and leveraged capital structure (D/E 139%).

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 0.91% is very low (<5%) but RISING +18.06% MoM — does not meet 'declining AND <5%' for pass; qualifies for borderline (<10%). Rising shorts on a high-quality name is worth monitoring (oral GLP-1 competition fears?).

ai_exposure.detail: AI is a meaningful tailwind for drug discovery (Lilly has invested heavily in AI-driven pipeline programs) and for diabetes/obesity care delivery. Not a direct AI revenue driver but accelerates pipeline economics. Neither a threat nor neutral.

deal_radar.assessment: FCF $9.2B against $43.4B debt and $5.3B cash — leveraged balance sheet from massive manufacturing buildout for GLP-1 capacity. D/E 139% is high for pharma but justified by demand visibility. Capex remains the dominant near-term cash use.

catalysts_general: Down 15.8% from 52-week high $1,133.95 — moderate drawdown after big run from $624 low
Mean analyst target $1,207.34 implies 26% upside; median $1,250 implies 31%
Forward P/E 21.7x against 169.9% earnings growth = PEG 1.33 — reasonable given pharma comp set typically trades 14-18x
24/31 analyst ratings are Buy/Strong Buy; recommendation_mean 1.71
Trading above SMA20 ($924.98) but below SMA50 ($941.92) — modest near-term consolidation
Beta 0.481 — defensive/low-volatility profile despite growth rate
Rising short interest (+18%) bears watching — likely tied to oral GLP-1 competitive concerns from NVO/PFE

downside_scenarios.scenario: Match analyst low target — Medicare GLP-1 reimbursement headwind or competitive launch from Pfizer's oral GLP-1.
Material GLP-1 market share loss to Novo's CagriSema or oral entrants.
Revisit 52-week low — broader pharma de-rating or major safety signal in GLP-1 class.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just below SMA20 ($924.98) and meaningfully below SMA50 ($941.92) — captures a small pullback within the larger uptrend. Better risk/reward against $1,207 mean target than chasing here. $920 also still respects the recent +2.76% 20d momentum.

comparables.note: Novo Nordisk — primary GLP-1 competitor; cheaper but with CagriSema execution risk and slower growth (not in input).
Merck — Keytruda concentration risk; far cheaper at far lower growth (not in input).
Pfizer — cheap but post-COVID cliff; potential GLP-1 oral competitor to LLY (not in input).

growth_picks.analyst_take: Eli Lilly scores 5.5/6 with the only borderline being rising short interest from a tiny base — likely competitive worries about oral GLP-1s but small in absolute terms. The 21.7x forward P/E against 170% earnings growth and 26% analyst upside is one of the most attractive risk/reward setups in mega-cap healthcare. The defensive beta (0.48) plus growth profile is a rare combination.
